LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MUSONDA KABWE'S MOTION TOVACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, IF ENTERED IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILING. CCP SEC. 473(B) REQUIRES THAT SUCH AN APPLICATION FOR RELIEF "SHALL BE ACCOMPANIED BY A COPY OF THE ANSWER OR OTHER PLEADING PROPOSED TO BE FILED THEREIN, OTHERWISE THE APPLICATION SHALL NOT BE GRANTED." ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

AMENDED POS RE: MTN TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IF ENTERED FILED BY DEFENDANT KABWE, MUSONDA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MUSONDA KABWE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT (CCP 473(B)). OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANT DID NOT SERVE AND FILE THE MOTION AT LEAST 16 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING, AS REQUIRED BY CODE CIV. PROC., 1005(B). IN VIEW OF THE STRONG POLICY FAVORING TRIAL OF CASES ON THEIR MERITS, AND DEFENDANT'S PROMPT ACTION IN MOVING TO SET ASIDE THE ORIGINAL DEFAULT, THE COURT URGES PLAINTIFF TO STIPULATE TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT TO AVOID ADDITIONAL UNNECESSARY MOTION PRACTICE. (SEE ELSTON V. CITY OF TURLOCK (1985) 38 CAL.3D 227, 233 [WHERE THE PARTY IN DEFAULT MOVES PROMPTLY TO SEEK RELIEF, AND NO PREJUDICE TO THE OPPOSING PARTY WILL RESULT FROM SETTING ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND LETTING THE CASE GO TO TRIAL ON THE MERITS, "VERY SLIGHT EVIDENCE WILL BE REQUIRED TO JUSTIFY A COURT IN SETTING ASIDE THE DEFAULT."]; SEE GENERALLY LASALLE V. VOGEL (2019) 36 CAL.APP.5TH 127.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default (Ccp 473(B))
Off Calendar Feb-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted on DEFENDANT MUSONDA KABWE's Motion To Set Aside Default (CCP 473(B)) as follows:

Defendant Musonda Kabwe's motion to set aside default is granted. Defendant shows that he reasonably believed that plaintiff would not pursue the action against him if he provided documentation that he was not the driver of the vehicle, which is sufficient evidence of mistake and excusable neglect under Code of Civil Procedure 473(b). Further, plaintiff's counsel did not notify defendant that he intended to take his default, and plaintiff has not shown that it would be prejudiced by setting aside the default. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233 [where the party in default moves promptly to seek relief, and no prejudice to the opposing party will result from setting aside the default and letting the case go to trial on the merits, "very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside the default."]; see generally Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127.)

Counsel for the defendant is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2019 Continued To Mar-24-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MUSONDA KABWE's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT (CCP 473(b)).

Off calendar. Defendant did not serve and file the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code Civ. Proc., 1005(b).

In view of the strong policy favoring trial of cases on their merits, and Defendant's prompt action in moving to set aside the original default, the Court urges Plaintiff to stipulate to set aside the default to avoid additional unnecessary motion practice. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233 [where the party in default moves promptly to seek relief, and no prejudice to the opposing party will result from setting aside the default and letting the case go to trial on the merits, "very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside the default."]; see generally Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Tovacate Default And Default Judgment, If Entered; Points And Authorities; Declaration
Off Calendar Nov-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lisa B. Meier, esq. (415-465-1266) specially appearing for Mark Nivinskus, counsel for Plaintiff.
Musonda Kabwe (916-627-8449) Defendant in propria persona.

Discussed. The Court notes there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule, and states the tentative ruling. Defendant stated he will file his papers. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MUSONDA KABWE's Motion Tovacate Default And Default Judgment, If Entered is denied without prejudice to refiling. CCP Sec. 473(b) requires that such an application for relief "shall be accompanied by a copy of the answer or other pleading proposed to be filed therein, otherwise the application shall not be granted."

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
